summary: Three of five held names carry live dilution or distribution overhangs the desk still reads as clean: NTSK (the book's best gainer, +$5.45) is being steadily distributed by its largest VC post-lockup (Lightspeed XII 219,075sh on 7/9 plus a ~1.65M-share director sale mid-June), while UUUU sits on an active $300M ATM and WRAP -- a failed +48% pump -- has a $200M S-3 baby-shelf ready; separately RIG, intel's top watchlist candidate, is hiding a dilutive ~$5.8B all-stock Valaris merger.

WRAP.note: Post-+48%-pump fade; position now +$1.09 at $2.11 but avg cost was walked down chasing weakness and the bot's own critic vetoed 3 further WRAP buys today for dilution-history/pump-signature. Correction to last snapshot: a $200M universal S-3 shelf IS on file for Wrap Technologies -- on a sub-$100M post-pump name that is standing baby-shelf dilution capacity, not an all-clear. No confirmed active 424B5/ATM draw yet. FinBERT mixed (mean 0.024, worst -0.853).

UUUU.note: Active $300M ATM (equity distribution agreement 2026-06-13, replacing the 2024 one) is a real ongoing dilution mechanism; entry thesis is CEO Bhappu's $968k (7/7) and chairman Hansen's (7/8) buys, but Lorentzian k-NN reads -0.75 (consistency 0.75) strongly bearish and position is -$1.34. Insider-buy thesis does not offset the ATM overhang.

CAI.note: Fundamentals clean (director $990k buy 7/13, Caris Detect launch, $100M buyback, no dilution filing). Held-ticker data-quality issue persists: nightly audit flags broker_disagrees (broker $17.50 vs cache $16.99, 3.0% drift). Broker is account of record so live P&L is fine, but any stop math referencing the cache is off -- unresolved, re-flagged a notch up for data reason not fundamentals.

NTSK.note: Position +$5.45 and +7.2% today, catalyst (52wk-high breakout, Wells Fargo/Jefferies/Cantor Buy) intact -- but post-IPO-lockup insider distribution is larger and more recent than last snapshot: Lightspeed Venture Partners XII sold 219,075sh on 7/9 (~$2.7M) after the 7/8 sale, on top of a ~1.65M-share Lightspeed director sale mid-June. Iconiq's $7.2M 7/8 buy partly offsets but net VC flow is distribution. FinBERT +0.158.

YEXT.note: No shelf/ATM/dilution filing. Failed Feb-2026 take-private replaced by an anti-dilutive $140M self-tender buyback; position +$0.93, no fresh negative catalyst.

flags.why: Intel/research keep RIG on the watchlist purely on the Equinor $1B+ backlog story, but there is a pending ~$5.8B all-stock acquisition of Valaris (15.235 RIG shares per Valaris share) that dilutes existing holders to ~53% of the combined company and is drawing legal scrutiny of the deal terms -- a material dilution plus binary shareholder-vote event, not the clean backlog trade intel presents.

Distressed reverse-merger pop, not organic strength: definitive Serapha Bio merger plus $230M concurrent private placement leaves pre-merger holders only ~3.7% of the combined entity; oncology pipeline halted, ~75% workforce cut. Still on today's scan (2.06) carrying a -3.0 internal penalty.

Most recent 10-Q discloses going-concern doubt including possible Chapter 7 liquidation; just closed a $23.7M private placement (warrant-overhang setup). Still on today's scan (0.1, -3.0 penalty); FinBERT -0.288 on the cash-runway story.

Top-3 scan candidate (2.96, below the 6.25 threshold) but severe dilution overhang: ~167M shares outstanding, an active $300M S-3 shelf, 13.95M warrants, authorized shares hiked to 450M. Phase 2 news is constructive but the capital structure is unresolved; on the desk avoid list.

Watchlist candidate but priced a $100M underwritten offering (13,495,277 sh at $7.41) on 6/24 -- real dilution alongside the real ABS-201 Phase 1 catalyst; stock ~$10.07 now sits above its mean analyst PT. Intel already declines a boost; keep flagged so it is not bought as a fresh breakout.

On today's scan (1.79) but distressed: down ~91% YoY near 52-wk lows despite a token insider buy, underwent a 10-for-1 reverse split this cycle tied to Nasdaq compliance, and is pivoting into Dogecoin mining hardware -- a distress signal, not a turnaround catalyst.
Automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 2026-01-13, already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM -- ongoing drip-dilution setup. On today's scan (1.83) carrying a -1.5 internal penalty.
Live $100M ATM (since 2024) plus a Feb-2026 $107.6M underwritten offering (20M sh at $5.00), now underwater near $3.73-3.83. On the desk avoid list; not held and not a current scan candidate.

dilution_watch.note: $300M at-the-market equity distribution agreement, actively usable now for this size producer; held position.
~$5.8B all-stock acquisition of Valaris (15.235 RIG sh per Valaris sh); existing RIG holders diluted to ~53% of combined co, deal terms under legal scrutiny.
$100M underwritten offering, 13,495,277 sh at $7.41; ~$6.08/sh immediate dilution.
Definitive Serapha Bio merger plus $230M concurrent private placement; existing holders diluted to ~3.7% pro forma.
$300M shelf plus 13.95M warrants and authorized shares hiked to 450M -- severe overhang.
$23.7M private placement; going-concern disclosure in most recent 10-Q.
Automatic shelf effective, used twice via 424B5; $50M ATM active.
$107.6M underwritten offering now underwater; $100M ATM remains active.

disagreements: RIG: intel.json and research.json keep RIG on the watchlist framed purely on the Equinor $1B+/7-rig-year backlog story, but neither mentions the pending ~$5.8B all-stock Valaris acquisition that dilutes existing RIG holders to ~53% of the combined company and is drawing legal challenge of the terms -- a material dilution and binary-vote risk sitting under a 'clean' watchlist name.

NTSK: intel reads net insider flow as 'mixed, not one-directional' on Iconiq's $7.2M buy, but the sell side is bigger and ongoing than that framing -- Lightspeed XII sold 219,075sh on 7/9 (~$2.7M) after the 7/8 sale, plus a ~1.65M-share Lightspeed director sale mid-June; post-lockup VC distribution is a live overhang the 'no negative news' read understates.

WRAP: intel repeats 'no new offering/ATM/shelf found,' but a $200M universal S-3 shelf IS on file for Wrap Technologies -- on a sub-$100M post-pump name that is baby-shelf dilution capacity standing ready, so 'no dilution mechanism' overstates the all-clear even without a confirmed 424B5 draw.

Discipline (carried): weekly buy budget shows 9/4 used per radar.html's execution checklist -- book is over the 4-buy weekly cap; surface as a standalone overtrading flag independent of any single ticker.

DVLT resolved: last snapshot's critical ledger-only DVLT flag now clears -- research.json records it manually closed 7/14 and broker_state.json (17:44Z) no longer reports the position; confirm the ledger reflects the close so P&L stops referencing an unconfirmed price.
